title: hypericum perfoliatum subsp. perfoliatum
intro: Hypericum perfoliatum subsp. perfoliatum, also known as the tall St. John's Wort or American St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family (Hypericaceae) native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Hypericum perfoliatum subsp. perfoliatum include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to improve mental health by reducing anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, and are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
